preset.geography.title: Geography
preset.materials.title: Materials
preset.cross-language.title: Cross-Language
search.hint.entity: Search for any movement, artist, artwork, museum, or theme.
search.hint.subject: Enter a subject such as dog, cat, flower, sea, or portrait to find artworks depicting it.
search.hint.nationality: Enter a country to discover artists from that place. This guided query is staged next.
search.hint.material: Enter a material or technique such as oil, marble, bronze, or watercolor. This guided query is staged next.
search.placeholder.subject: Artworks depicting dog, cat, flower...
search.placeholder.material: Works made with oil, marble, bronze...
modeSummary.observatory.explanation: Observatory mode keeps the semantic field central while the Query Mirror remains continuously visible.
modeSummary.query-theater.explanation: Query Theater makes the GraphQL document itself a primary storytelling surface for demos and teaching.
modeSummary.polyglot-studio.explanation: Polyglot Studio foregrounds language variation, multilingual labels, and source-aware semantic surfaces.
modeSummary.temporal-river.explanation: Temporal River privileges chronology, succession, and influence flow while reusing the same GraphQL backbone.
modeSummary.recit.explanation: Recit mode presents the selected entity as a readable narrative, with no graph required.
languageSurface.fr.primary: Rechercher un mouvement, un artiste, une oeuvre...
languageSurface.fr.secondary: Trace a movement, artist, work, or place...
languageSurface.en.primary: Trace a movement, artist, work, or place...
languageSurface.en.secondary: Explorer un courant, un createur, une oeuvre...
compassLens.movement.title: Movement
compassLens.movement.summary: Movement-anchored view for succession, schools, and artistic branches.
compassLens.movement.copy: The compass brings movements forward: prioritize movement nodes and succession lines to read the evolution of styles.
compassLens.artist.title: Artist
compassLens.artist.summary: Creator-centered view for influence and artwork trajectories.
compassLens.artist.copy: Emphasize artists to follow who creates, who influences, and how artwork branches unfold.
compassLens.work.title: Work
compassLens.work.summary: Work-focused view for subjects, materials, and display locations.
compassLens.work.copy: This lens brings works and their concrete attachments forward: subjects, materials, museums, and creation links.
compassLens.place.title: Place
compassLens.place.summary: Place-oriented view for museums, display contexts, and geography.
compassLens.place.copy: Use this lens to read where works are situated and where graph branches meet places.
compassLens.time.title: Time
compassLens.time.summary: Time-weighted view for dated entities, overlap, and period reading.
compassLens.time.copy: The time lens highlights dated nodes so the observatory already hints at Temporal River style reading.
compassLens.influence.title: Influence
compassLens.influence.summary: Influence-first view for lineage, succession, and transmission.
compassLens.influence.copy: Choose this lens to foreground influence links and succession between movements and artists.
lensPreset.lineage.summary: Active preset: Lineage. Movements, influence, and succession become the main reading.
lensPreset.lineage.copy: Lineage gathers movement and influence trajectories to show how one branch precedes or feeds another.
lensPreset.geography.summary: Active preset: Geography. Museums, places, and diffusion reframe the reading.
lensPreset.geography.copy: Geography surfaces places and collections to read circulation and spatial anchors across the graph.
lensPreset.materials.summary: Active preset: Materials. Works, subjects, and materials structure the scene.
lensPreset.materials.copy: Materials favors the concrete branches of works: what they depict, what they are made of, and where they are seen.
lensPreset.cross-language.summary: Active preset: Cross-Language. Multilingual labels become the comparison surface.
lensPreset.cross-language.copy: Cross-Language does not aggressively filter the graph: it mainly invites you to compare FR and EN surfaces for the same entities.
heroAction.observatory.1.title: Read the Constellation
heroAction.observatory.1.copy: Start by clicking a movement, artist, or work in the center. Read this view as the main semantic field: nodes are the visible entities and links are the active relationships.
heroAction.observatory.2.title: Switch to Query Theater
heroAction.observatory.2.copy: Use this mode when you want to understand the live query. Read the Query Mirror as an execution trace: document, variables, response shape, then graph update.
heroAction.observatory.3.title: Switch to Temporal River
heroAction.observatory.3.copy: Use this mode to interpret the graph through periods, succession, and influence. The temporal rail shows the active window and the constellation highlights the entities that belong to that slice.
heroAction.query-theater.1.title: Choose an Entity
heroAction.query-theater.1.copy: Click an entity in the constellation to trigger a query. The center stays the main stage, but attention shifts to the active GraphQL document.
heroAction.query-theater.2.title: Read the Query Trace
heroAction.query-theater.2.copy: Interpret the left panels as a teaching trace: Session Flow shows call order, Variables shows the input, and Response Shape shows the returned structure.
heroAction.query-theater.3.title: Interpret the Update
heroAction.query-theater.3.copy: Compare the active document with the new nodes and links. When the graph changes, the query has opened a new semantic branch in the observatory.
heroAction.temporal-river.1.title: Set a Time Window
heroAction.temporal-river.1.copy: Select a movement, artist, or dated work to establish a time window. The lower rail becomes the main reference for reading the scene.
heroAction.temporal-river.2.title: Read the Flow
heroAction.temporal-river.2.copy: Interpret Temporal Focus, Active Entities, and Flow as a chronological summary. They show the active range, overlapping entities, and visible succession or influence paths.
heroAction.temporal-river.3.title: Compare Center and Time
heroAction.temporal-river.3.copy: Bright nodes belong to the active window, while muted nodes sit outside it. Read the constellation as a temporal map rather than only a general graph.
heroAction.polyglot-studio.1.title: Compare Languages
heroAction.polyglot-studio.1.copy: Switch between FR and EN to see how the same entities change linguistic surface without changing Wikidata identity.
heroAction.polyglot-studio.2.title: Read the Labels
heroAction.polyglot-studio.2.copy: Interpret the polyglot cards as multiple readings of the same source. A label change does not mean a new entity, only a new reading surface.
heroAction.polyglot-studio.3.title: Relate Language and Source
heroAction.polyglot-studio.3.copy: Keep an eye on the active GraphQL document while the language changes. This shows the multilingual layer and the query layer working together.
queryNarrative.movement_details: Movement details for {target} keep start dates, end dates, and succession visible in the observatory.
queryNarrative.movement_evolution: Movement evolution for {target} traces predecessors, successors, and broader lineage.
queryNarrative.artists_by_movement: Artists linked to {target} expand the constellation from movement into people and dated careers.
queryNarrative.artist_details: Artist details for {target} bring lifespan and movement membership into the visible field.
queryNarrative.artist_influences: Artist influence flow for {target} turns inspiration into readable graph and river edges.
queryNarrative.artworks_by_artist: Works by {target} translate an artist selection into a creation timeline.
queryNarrative.artwork_details: Artwork details for {target} keep creation date, museum, and subjects visible with the active query.
queryNarrative.artworks_by_museum: Museum expansion for {target} shows holdings as a live GraphQL collection.
queryNarrative.artworks_by_subject: Subject expansion for {target} reveals artworks that share a semantic theme.
queryNarrative.movements_catalog: Movement catalog retrieval provides the broad temporal field for timeline and river views.
queryNarrative.entity_label: Multilingual labels for {target} keep the same Wikidata entity readable across French and English surfaces.
timeline.activeEntities.none: No dated entities overlap the active window
timeline.flow.fallback: {first} to {last} keeps chronology visible even before richer influence edges load.
recit.empty: Select a movement, artist, or work to read its story.
recit.movement.intro.country: {label} is an art movement born in {country}.
recit.movement.intro.generic: {label} is a major art movement in art history.
recit.artist.intro.generic: {label} is a major artist in art history.
recit.artwork.intro.creatorDate: {label} is a work by {creator}, made in {year}.
recit.artwork.intro.creator: {label} is a work by {creator}.
recit.artwork.intro.generic: {label} is a notable work in art history.
shell.brand.title: MotusArtium
shell.brand.subtitle: Polyglot query observatory for tracing movements, artists, works, places, and influence across art history.
shell.hero.title: Navigate art history through live query surfaces and multilingual semantics.
shell.hero.description: MotusArtium is designed as a cultural instrument rather than a dashboard: a place where GraphQL documents, semantic relationships, and language-aware exploration are visible at the same time.
shell.entry.movements.title: Start with Movements
shell.entry.movements.copy: Enter through schools, ruptures, and successions to watch art history reorganize itself as a living constellation.
shell.entry.artists.title: Start with Artists
shell.entry.artists.copy: Trace creation through people, influence, birthplace, and oeuvre while the active GraphQL path remains visible.
shell.entry.themes.title: Start with Themes
shell.entry.themes.copy: Approach the atlas by material, subject, museum, or place and let the interface reshape the narrative lens.
shell.appRoot.hint: Multilingual mount: this panel is reserved for embedded Multilingual UI fragments and diagnostics as we progressively connect more of the original runtime into the Observatory shell.
shell.polyglot.card1.title: Interface Surface
shell.polyglot.card1.copy: The shell changes prompts, labels, and interpretation text with the active language.
shell.polyglot.card2.title: Entity Surface
shell.polyglot.card2.copy: Selected entities can be read through different language surfaces while keeping the same Wikidata identity.
shell.polyglot.card3.title: Source Lens
recit.generic.empty: Select an entity to read its story.

comparison.title: Comparison
comparison.waiting: Select another entity to compare with {label}.
comparison.tip: Use the + Compare button in search results.
